div1.entryFree: Tutor, subst. one who teaches and instructs, a master: thy eyes' shrewd t., that hard heart of thine, hath taught them scornful tricks, Ven. 500. O time, thou t. both to good and bad, teach me to curse him, Lucr. 995 (cf. the verb in R2 IV, 166). Tp. I, 2, 174. 469 (my foot my t.?). Gent. II, 1, 144. III, 1, 84. LLL IV, 3, 323 (beauty's --s, i. e. those who taught you what beauty was). Shr. II, 111. H4B V, 5, 66. H6C I, 3, 2. Troil. II, 3, 32. Troil. II, 3, 32 Tit. V, 1, 98. Hml. III, 2, 19. Lr. III, 2, 83 (when nobles are their tailors' --s, i. e. are able to instruct them in their art, are their masters).
